violations.text: Not all foods served potentially raw or undercooked on the menu were marked with an asterisk. Per the PIC, new menus to be made will provide asterisks as needed. (At the time of re-inspection, PIC stated new menus currently in process for review and printing.) New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Dish machine was observed not moving sanitizer liquid through the tubing. All equipment/utensils are to be sanitized properly at the 3-bay sink until unit is adjusted/repaired. Per the PIC, the kitchen high temp dish machine is to be used for bar equipment until serviced/repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026

Garlic butters were observed held in refrigeration beyond 7 days from the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be held no longer than 7 days from the marked date of preparation unless frozen. Garlic butters were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026
Not all foods served potentially raw or undercooked on the menu were marked with an asterisk. Per the PIC, new menus to be made will provide asterisks as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026

At the time of inspection, reduced Oxygen packaged fish was observed thawing int he walk-in cooler at 31 degrees Fahrenheit. REDUCED OXYGEN PACKAGED FISH that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. Based on temperature observed, fish was removed from packaging at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (with containers in bar bottle holder) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location to prevent possible contamination of the ice scoop food contact surface. Scoop(s) was removed for sanitization and properly stored at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (salad) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. PIC, to review glove wearing with staff. Salad was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023

Marinara sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Sauce was prepared yesterday and observed at 48 degrees. PIC, voluntarily disposed of food. Proper cooling procedures with PIC. PIC to inform staff of proper cooling procedures. Cooling methods handout provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

ROP muscles were observed thawing with package in tact. Muscles were observed at 36 degrees. Package was sliced open. PIC to review with staff proper thawing procedures for ALL ROP seafood. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023
Multiple knives were observed stored between prep units/wall. Knives were moved. Utensils must be stored in/on a clean surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of multiple ice machines have an accumulation of bio-film. Inspector recommended switching to a more frequent routine cleaning schedule. PIC stated ice machines would emptied and cleaned by the end of the day. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023
